option_2: Cryptography
option_3: Metaverse
option_4: Virtual matrix
solution_text:
Explanation : (C)

  •  The most appropriate word/phrase to describe the given concept is “metaverse”.
  •  The term “metaverse” refers to an interconnected network of 3D virtual worlds that can be accessed by millions of users simultaneously.
  •  The metaverse is a shared environment that spans a multitude of 3D virtual worlds. option (c) is the correct answer.

question:
With reference to Unmanned Aerial Vehicles (UAVs), consider the following statements: (UPSC Prelims 2025)

I. All types of UAVs can do vertical landing.

II. All types of UAVs can do automated hovering.

III. All types of UAVs can use battery only as a source of power supply.

How many of the statements given above are correct?
option_1: Only one
option_2: Only two
option_3: All the three
option_4: None
solution_text:
Explanation: (d) None

  • Only certain types of UAVs (like quadcopters or VTOL-enabled UAVs) can perform vertical landing.
  • Fixed-wing UAVs generally require a runway.
  • All types of UAVs can't do automated hovering. Only rotary-wing UAVs (like drones with rotors) can hover. Fixed-wing UAVs cannot hover; they require continuous forward motion to stay aloft.
  • Larger UAVs often use gasoline, hybrid systems, or solar.

question:
In the context of electric vehicle batteries, consider the following elements: (UPSC Prelims 2025)

I. Cobalt

II. Graphite

III. Lithium

IV. Nickel

How many of the above usually make up battery cathodes?
option_1: Only one
option_2: Only two
option_3: Only three
option_4: All the four
solution_text:
Explanation: (c) Only three

  • Cobalt : Enhances stability and prevents overheating. Widely used in Lithium Cobalt Oxide (LCO) and NMC (Nickel Manganese Cobalt) batteries.
  • Lithium : Forms the base of all lithium-ion batteries and is essential in the cathode structure.
  • Nickel : Improves energy density and storage capacity. Found in NMC and NCA (Nickel Cobalt Aluminum) batteries.
  • Graphite - This is not used in the cathode. It serves as the most common material for the anode, where lithium ions are stored during charging.

question:
Consider the following: (UPSC Prelims 2025)

I. Cigarette butts

II. Eyeglass lenses

III. Car tyres

How many of them contain plastic?
option_1: Only one
option_2: Only two
option_3: All the three
option_4: None
solution_text:
Explanation: (c) All the three

  • Cigarette butts : The filter is made from cellulose acetate, a type of plastic. They are one of the most littered plastic pollutants worldwide.
  • Eyeglass lenses : Most modern lenses are made from polycarbonate or CR-39 plastic, not glass, due to their lightweight and shatter-resistant properties.
  • Car tyres : In addition to natural and synthetic rubber, tyres contain plastic-based polymers like styrene-butadiene rubber, which is a synthetic plastic compound.

question:
Consider the following statements: (UPSC Prelims 2025)

I. It is expected that Majorana 1 chip will enable quantum computing.

II. Majorana 1 chip has been introduced by Amazon Web Services (AWS).

III. Deep learning is a subset of machine learning.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
option_1: I and II only
option_2: II and III only
option_3: I and III only
option_4: I, II and III
solution_text:
Explanation: (c) I and III only

  • The Majorana 1 chip is part of the ongoing research into quantum computing and is associated with the use of Majorana fermions exotic particles that may help in creating more stable quantum bits (qubits). This chip is expected to contribute to the advancement of quantum computers. It is being developed by Microsoft, not AWS.
  • Deep learning is a specialized field within machine learning that uses neural networks with many layers to model complex patterns in data.

question:
With reference to monoclonal antibodies, often mentioned in news, consider the following statements: (UPSC Prerlims 2025)

I. They are man-made proteins.

II. They stimulate immunological function due to their ability to bind to specific antigens.

III. They are used in treating viral infections like that of Nipah virus.

Which of the statements given above are correct?
option_1: I and II only
option_2: II and III only
option_3: I and III only
option_4: I, II and III
solution_text:
Explanation: (d) I, II and III

  • Monoclonal antibodies are artificially engineered proteins designed to mimic the immune system's ability to fight harmful pathogens like viruses. They are produced by identical immune cells cloned from a single parent cell, making them highly specific to particular antigens.
  • By binding to these antigens, they can directly neutralize pathogens or mark them for destruction by other immune cells, thereby enhancing the immune response.
  • They have been increasingly used in treating various viral infections, including experimental treatments for diseases like Ebola and Nipah virus, where conventional antiviral drugs may not exist.

question:
Consider the following statements: (UPSC Prelims 2025)

I. No virus can survive in ocean waters.

II. No virus can infect bacteria.

III. No virus can change the cellular transcriptional activity in host cells.

How many of the statements given above are correct?
option_1: Only one
option_2: Only two
option_3: All the three
option_4: None
solution_text:
Explanation: (d) None

  • Viruses can and do survive in ocean waters. In fact, oceans contain vast numbers of viruses, particularly bacteriophages, which play a role in marine ecosystems.
  • Bacteriophages are viruses that specifically infect bacteria. They are extensively studied in genetics and molecular biology and even explored as alternatives to antibiotics.
  • Many viruses alter the host cell's transcriptional machinery to promote their own replication. For instance, HIV and various DNA viruses hijack and modulate host transcriptional processes for viral gene expression.

question:
Consider the following space missions: (UPSC Prelims 2025)

I. Axiom-4

II. SpaDeX

III. Gaganyaan

How many of the space missions given above encourage and support micro-gravity research?
option_1: Only one
option_2: Only two
option_3: All three
option_4: None
solution_text:
Explanation: (b) Only two

  • Axiom-4: A private space mission to the International Space Station (ISS), supports micro-gravity research as part of its objectives.
  • SpaDeX (Space Docking Experiment): Conducted by ISRO to demonstrate autonomous docking capabilities. Its primary goal is technology demonstration, not scientific experimentation in microgravity.
  • Gaganyaan: India's human spaceflight mission will include experiments in micro-gravity during crewed missions.

question: Artificial way of causing rainfall to reduce air pollution makes use of.................   (UPSC Prelims 2025)
option_1: Silver iodide and potassium iodide
option_2: Silver nitrate and potassium iodide
option_3: Silver iodide and potassium nitrate
option_4: Silver nitrate and potassium chloride
solution_text:
Explanation: (a) Silver iodide and potassium iodide

  • Artificial rainfall (cloud seeding) is typically carried out using substances like silver iodide and potassium iodide.
  • These substances act as cloud condensation or ice nuclei, encouraging the formation of raindrops in clouds.
  • Silver nitrate and potassium nitrate are not used for this purpose.

question:
GPS-Aided Geo Augmented Navigation (GAGAN) uses a system of ground stations to provide necessary augmentation. Which of the following statements is/are correct in respect of GAGAN? (UPSC Prelims 2025)

I. It is designed to provide additional accuracy and integrity.

II. It will allow more uniform and high-quality air traffic management.

III. It will provide benefits only in aviation but not in other modes of transportation.

Select the correct answer using the code given below:
option_1: I, II and III
option_2: II and III only
option_3: I only
option_4: I and II only
solution_text:
Explanation: (d) I and II only

  • Statement I is correct: GAGAN (GPS Aided GEO Augmented Navigation) is designed to provide additional accuracy, integrity, and reliability to GPS signals, improving positioning services.
  • Statement II is correct: By improving navigation accuracy, GAGAN enables more efficient and uniform air traffic management, especially in congested or difficult terrains.
  • Statement III is incorrect: While GAGAN was primarily developed for aviation, it is also beneficial for other sectors like railways, maritime, agriculture, and surveying.
